conditions: Closest 4 ft piling at right edge is very sharp with clear encrusting organisms. The 11 ft piling to its left is visible as a distinct structure but with reduced detail. The 14 ft/back-left region is present but mostly as a darker silhouette with little texture. No clear view of 30 ft pilings or sandy bottom. This corresponds to conditions where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded, indicating about 15 ft visibility.

Closest 4 ft piling at right is sharp with clear texture. The mid/right and back-left pilings (~11–14 ft and beyond) are only faint silhouettes with little detail, and the seafloor is not visible. This matches the criterion where the 14 ft piling is a hazy silhouette rather than sharp.

The sandy bottom is clearly visible and multiple rows of pilings including those around 30 ft away show distinct edges and some texture, comparable to the exceptional reference rather than the hazier 25 ft case.

All pilings including the far ~30 ft ones are visible with fairly good texture detail, and the sandy bottom is clearly seen across the frame. Conditions are very similar to the exceptional 35 ft reference, so visibility is at the top of the scale.

Closest 4 ft piling on right is very sharp with clear texture. The 11 ft piling is visible but hazy, and the 14 ft/30 ft pilings are only vague vertical bands with no clear structure, similar to or slightly better than the 25 ft reference silhouettes but without bottom visible. This corresponds best to the guideline where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded.

The closest 4ft piling at right is sharp with good texture. The mid/right piling and a farther central piling are visible but both appear as soft silhouettes with little detail. This matches conditions where the 14ft+ pilings are hazy/faded rather than crisp, indicating around 15 ft visibility.
